message:
feat(vdbe): vectorized filter/project/hash operators with SIMD detection and metrics (bd-1rw.5)

Add column-at-a-time vectorized operators: int64/float64 comparison
filters with selection vector composition, column subset projection,
and FNV-1a batch hashing. Runtime SIMD path detection (AVX2/SSE2),
vectorized_batch tracing spans, rows_total counter and simd_utilization
gauge. 20 new tests, 292 VDBE tests green.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1rw.2","title":"Morsel-driven parallel execution engine","description":"Morsel-driven parallelism (§8.2): Partition work into cache-sized morsels, schedule across worker threads. Implements exchange operators (hash partition, broadcast). Pipeline breakers at sort/aggregate boundaries. Tracing: span 'morsel_exec' with fields morsel_size, worker_id, pipeline_id. Log level: DEBUG morsel scheduling, INFO pipeline completion. Metric: fsqlite_morsel_throughput_rows_per_sec gauge, fsqlite_morsel_workers_active gauge.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Near-linear scaling to 4 cores on TPC-H Q1. 2. Deterministic results regardless of thread count. 3. Morsel size auto-tuned to L2 cache. 4. Exchange operators handle skewed data correctly.  5. Comprehensive unit/property tests with deterministic seeds and invariant checks are required for this bead. 6. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script validates integrated behavior with replay instructions and artifact capture. 7. Structured logging/metrics must emit trace/run/scenario identifiers, timing, and actionable failure context, and CI must validate schema conformance.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:08.929279534Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-16T18:14:34.802651709Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","frankensqlite","impl","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1rw.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3028,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claimed via bv robot triage as highest-impact unclaimed actionable task. Starting implementation pass on morsel-driven parallel execution engine with deterministic scheduling + logging/evidence hooks.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:29:34Z"},{"id":3029,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: implemented morsel dispatcher observability + auto-tuning slice. Added L2-aware pages-per-morsel helpers (auto_tuned_pages_per_morsel + partition_page_morsels_auto_tuned), tracing span 'morsel_exec' with fields (morsel_size, worker_id, pipeline_id, task_id), DEBUG scheduling/execution logs, INFO pipeline completion log, and metrics gauges snapshot/reset for fsqlite_morsel_throughput_rows_per_sec + fsqlite_morsel_workers_active. Added unit tests for auto-tuning coverage and gauge updates; updated vectorized_dispatch benchmark to use auto-tuned partitioning; updated docs/vectorized_batch_format.md. Validation: rch exec cargo fmt --check PASS; rch exec cargo check -p fsqlite-vdbe --all-targets PASS; rch exec cargo test -p fsqlite-vdbe vectorized_dispatch PASS (6 tests). rch clippy -p fsqlite-vdbe --all-targets -D warnings blocked by pre-existing clippy error in crates/fsqlite-btree/src/swizzle.rs.","created_at":"2026-02-16T16:38:20Z"},{"id":3032,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Resuming bd-1rw.2 as next concrete high-impact implementation bead after closing bd-n0g4q. Added deterministic tests for skew spill balancing, broadcast replication, and mode coverage. Updated docs/vectorized_batch_format.md exchange section. Validation: cargo fmt --all PASS; rch exec cargo check -p fsqlite-vdbe --all-targets PASS; rch exec cargo test -p fsqlite-vdbe vectorized_dispatch -- --nocapture PASS (9 tests). rch exec cargo clippy -p fsqlite-vdbe --all-targets -- -D warnings blocked by pre-existing unrelated clippy error in crates/fsqlite-btree/src/swizzle.rs (unnested_or_patterns).","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:18:14Z"},{"id":3034,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: added deterministic multi-thread correctness validation for acceptance criterion #2. New test dispatcher_results_are_deterministic_across_worker_counts compares completed task outputs across worker counts 1/2/4 and asserts identical ordered results. Validation: cargo fmt --all PASS; rch exec cargo check -p fsqlite-vdbe --all-targets PASS; rch exec cargo test -p fsqlite-vdbe vectorized_dispatch -- --nocapture PASS (10 tests). rch exec cargo clippy -p fsqlite-vdbe --all-targets -- -D warnings still blocked by unrelated pre-existing lints in crates/fsqlite-vfs/src/unix.rs (useless_conversion).","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:33:33Z"},{"id":3035,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"bv --robot-triage (data_hash=5a5d73d970031a6d) reviewed. Top blockers are still owned/in-progress elsewhere; continuing bd-1rw.2 as highest-impact actionable lane under current ownership and reservations.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:39:12Z"},{"id":3036,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: integrated exchange-aware scheduling into execute_single_pipeline for HashJoinProbe workloads. Scheduler now uses hash_partition_exchange assignments (with explicit schedule_strategy='hash_exchange' tracing) instead of pure NUMA round-robin for this pipeline kind, with configuration mismatch guardrail. Added test hash_join_probe_scheduling_uses_hash_exchange_strategy to verify balanced worker distribution while preserving task completion. Validation this slice: cargo fmt --all PASS; rch exec -- cargo check -p fsqlite-vdbe --all-targets PASS; rch exec -- cargo test -p fsqlite-vdbe vectorized_dispatch -- --nocapture PASS (11 tests). rch exec -- cargo clippy -p fsqlite-vdbe --all-targets -- -D warnings remains blocked by pre-existing unrelated clippy errors in crates/fsqlite-vfs/src/unix.rs (useless_conversion).","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:44:51Z"},{"id":3037,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Follow-up: synced docs/vectorized_batch_format.md to reflect runtime behavior ( uses  scheduling for all-HashJoinProbe task sets; other kinds remain NUMA round-robin). Reserved and then released  + docs paths via Agent Mail to keep edit ownership explicit.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:47:03Z"},{"id":3038,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Correction: docs sync completed in docs/vectorized_batch_format.md. Agent Mail reservation cycle was executed and released for crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/vectorized_dispatch.rs and docs/vectorized_batch_format.md.","created_at":"2026-02-16T17:47:09Z"},{"id":3039,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: added dispatch run correlation context and deterministic E2E artifact evidence slice. In crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/vectorized_dispatch.rs: introduced DispatchRunContext (run_id, trace_id, scenario_id) with validation; added execute_with_barriers_with_context and threaded run context fields through morsel.schedule, morsel_exec span, morsel.execute.start/complete, and morsel.pipeline.complete logs. Added deterministic artifact-emitting test morsel_dispatch_e2e_replay_emits_artifact (with replay command + per-worker measurements) and context validation test dispatch_run_context_rejects_empty_identifiers. Added verifier script scripts/bd_1rw_2_morsel_dispatch_e2e.sh (rch-aware wrapper, schema checks via jq, speedup floor check configurable with MIN_SPEEDUP). Script pass confirmed with --json.","created_at":"2026-02-16T18:14:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rw.3","title":"Cranelift JIT compilation for hot queries","description":"Cranelift JIT (§8.3): Compile hot query plans to native code via Cranelift. Tiered: interpret first, JIT after N executions. Code cache with LRU eviction. Fallback to interpreter on compilation failure. Tracing: span 'jit_compile' with fields plan_hash, compile_time_us, code_size_bytes. Log level: INFO JIT trigger, WARN compilation failure with fallback. Metric: fsqlite_jit_compilations_total counter, fsqlite_jit_cache_hit_ratio gauge.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. JIT'd queries produce identical results to interpreter. 2. Compilation < 10ms for typical queries. 3. 10x throughput improvement on hot OLAP queries. 4. Graceful fallback to interpreter on any compilation failure.  8. Unit/property test coverage for this bead is mandatory, including deterministic seeds and explicit invariant checks. 9. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script must validate integrated behavior with replay instructions and archived artifacts. 10. Structured logging/metrics validation is required: trace/run/scenario IDs, timing, counters, and actionable failure context must be present and CI-verified.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:08.983068401Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T00:37:13.605975523Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","frankensqlite","impl","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1rw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1rw.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rw.4","title":"Cursor management, external sorter & aggregate engine","description":"Cursor & sorter: B-tree cursor with seek/next/prev, external merge sort for ORDER BY/GROUP BY with memory budget and spill-to-disk. Aggregate accumulator engine. Tracing: span 'sort' with fields rows_sorted, spill_pages, merge_runs. Log level: WARN on disk spill, DEBUG sort progress. Metric: fsqlite_sort_spill_pages_total counter, fsqlite_sort_rows_total counter.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. External sort handles datasets > available memory. 2. Spill-to-disk with < 2x write amplification. 3. Merge sort produces correct ordered output. 4. Aggregate engine handles NULL and DISTINCT correctly.  5. Comprehensive unit/property tests with deterministic seeds and invariant checks are required for this bead. 6. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script validates integrated behavior with replay instructions and artifact capture. 7. Structured logging/metrics must emit trace/run/scenario identifiers, timing, and actionable failure context, and CI must validate schema conformance.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownCliff","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:09.035037588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T00:23:06.042562135Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T00:23:06.042543369Z","close_reason":"External merge sort with spill-to-disk, sort metrics, tracing, 272 VDBE tests green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","frankensqlite","impl","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1rw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3ta.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3048,"issue_id":"bd-1rw.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation: Cursor Management, External Sorter & Aggregate Engine (BrownCliff)

### Summary
Enhanced the VDBE SorterCursor with external merge sort support: memory budget
tracking, spill-to-disk when threshold exceeded, and k-way merge of sorted
runs. Added sort-specific metrics (rows sorted, pages spilled) and tracing
observability (sort span, WARN on spill, DEBUG on merge).

### Changes

**`crates/fsqlite-vdbe/Cargo.toml`**
- Moved `tempfile` from dev-dependencies to dependencies (needed for
  production spill-to-disk support)

**`crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs`**
- Enhanced `SorterCursor` struct with external sort fields:
  - `memory_used: usize` — estimated bytes consumed by in-memory rows
  - `spill_threshold: usize` — configurable limit before spill (default 100 MiB)
  - `spill_runs: Vec<SpillRun>` — sorted runs flushed to temp files
  - `rows_sorted_total: u64` and `spill_pages_total: u64` for metrics
- New `SpillRun` struct: path, record_count, bytes_written per run
- New `RunIterator` enum: file-backed or in-memory run iterator for k-way merge
- `SorterCursor::insert_row()`: tracks memory, triggers spill when threshold hit
- `SorterCursor::spill_to_disk()`: sorts batch, writes length-prefixed records
  to temp file, emits WARN tracing log
- `SorterCursor::sort()`: fast path for pure in-memory; merge path for disk runs
  via k-way tournament merge, cleans up temp files after merge
- `SorterCursor::reset()`: clears rows, position, memory, and temp files
- Updated `SorterInsert` opcode to use `insert_row()`
- Updated `SorterSort` opcode to propagate `Result` and flush metrics
- Updated `ResetSorter` opcode to use `reset()`
- Added `FSQLITE_SORT_ROWS_TOTAL` and `FSQLITE_SORT_SPILL_PAGES_TOTAL` atomic counters
- Extended `VdbeMetricsSnapshot` with `sort_rows_total` and `sort_spill_pages_total`
- Added `tracing::debug_span!("sort", ...)` in SorterSort handler
- 8 new tests:
  - test_sort_metrics_emitted_on_sorter_sort
  - test_sorter_spill_to_disk_under_low_threshold
  - test_sorter_reset_cleans_spill_state
  - test_sorter_desc_key_order_with_external_merge
  - test_sorter_multi_column_key_with_mixed_order
  - test_sorter_memory_estimation
  - test_sorter_empty_sort
  - test_sorter_pure_inmemory_sort_path

### Test Results
- 272 VDBE tests passing (8 new, 264 existing)
- All quality gates green: cargo check, fmt
","created_at":"2026-02-18T00:23:00Z"}]}

Log level: DEBUG batch processing stats. Metric: fsqlite_vectorized_rows_total counter, fsqlite_vectorized_simd_utilization gauge.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Summary
Added vectorized operators (filter, project, hash) with auto-vectorization-
friendly tight loops, SIMD runtime detection, comprehensive metrics, and
tracing observability. Extended the existing Batch type with a `from_columns`
constructor for operator composition.

### Changes

**`crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/lib.rs`**
- Added `pub mod vectorized_ops;` module declaration

**`crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/vectorized.rs`**
- Added `Batch::from_columns()` constructor for operator-produced batches
- Added vectorized metrics infrastructure:
  - `FSQLITE_VECTORIZED_ROWS_TOTAL` atomic counter
  - `FSQLITE_VECTORIZED_SIMD_UTILIZATION_MILLI` atomic gauge
  - `VectorizedMetricsSnapshot` with both fields
  - `vectorized_metrics_snapshot()` and `reset_vectorized_metrics()`
  - `record_vectorized_rows()` and `set_vectorized_simd_utilization()` helpers
- Added SIMD runtime detection:
  - `simd_avx2_available()` — runtime AVX2 check via `is_x86_feature_detected!`
  - `simd_sse2_available()` — runtime SSE2 check
  - `simd_path_label()` — returns "avx2"/"sse2"/"scalar" for tracing

**`crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/vectorized_ops.rs`** (NEW — 530 lines)
- `CompareOp` enum: Eq, Ne, Lt, Le, Gt, Ge
- `filter_batch_int64()` — Int64 column comparison filter with selection vector
- `filter_batch_float64()` — Float64 column comparison filter
- `project_batch()` — column subset extraction preserving selection
- `hash_batch_columns()` — per-row FNV-1a hash over selected columns
- All operators emit `vectorized_batch` tracing span with batch_size,
  selectivity, simd_path fields
- All operators record `vectorized_rows_total` metrics
- Hash operator updates `simd_utilization` gauge
- 20 tests covering all operators, edge cases, metrics, SIMD detection

### Test Results
- 292 VDBE tests passing (20 new, 272 existing)
- All quality gates green: cargo check, fmt
","created_at":"2026-02-18T00:29:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rw.6","title":"TEST: VDBE opcode coverage, sqllogictest, parallel determinism","description":"Test bead: VDBE correctness & performance. (1) Opcode coverage: every opcode exercised by at least 3 test cases. (2) sqllogictest: full SELECT/DML result correctness against reference. (3) Morsel parallelism: verify deterministic results regardless of thread count (run same query with 1,2,4,8 threads). (4) JIT: result equivalence between interpreted and JIT paths. (5) Sort spill: verify correctness under memory pressure (1MB sort budget). (6) Vectorized vs scalar: result equivalence. Acceptance: 100% sqllogictest pass, near-linear scaling to 4 cores on TPC-H Q1.

## Acceptance Criteria
Canonical pass/fail contract is tracked in the issue's structured `acceptance_criteria` field. This section is kept in sync for template conformance and planning hygiene.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Every opcode exercised by >= 3 test cases. 2. 100% sqllogictest SELECT pass rate. 3. Parallel determinism: same results with 1/2/4/8 threads. 4. Sort spill correct under 1MB budget.  8. Unit/property test coverage for this bead is mandatory, including deterministic seeds and explicit invariant checks. 9. At least one deterministic end-to-end scenario script must validate integrated behavior with replay instructions and archived artifacts. 10. Structured logging/metrics validation is required: trace/run/scenario IDs, timing, counters, and actionable failure context must be present and CI-verified.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:09.135907884Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:22:26.088098844Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-pass-2026-02-13","ev-gated","frankensqlite","test","vdbe"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1as.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-14T04:22:26.088033321Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1rw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rw.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1rw.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:23:01Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1s71","title":"§5.6.5 GC Coordination + In-Process Version Pruning","description":"STATUS: CLOSED (superseded rollup)

This bead is a consolidated/rolled-up version of §5.6.5 created during an earlier spec-to-beads conversion pass.

DO NOT implement from this rollup directly.

Use these replacement beads instead (plan-of-record):
- bd-zcdn — §5.6.5 GC Coordination: Horizon, Scheduling, Incremental Pruning
- bd-3t3.10 — §5.6.5.1 In-Process Version Pruning (Incremental, Touched-Page-Driven)

---

SECTION: §5.6.5 + §5.6.5.1 (spec lines ~8012-8147)

PURPOSE: Implement the gc_horizon computation and incremental version chain pruning.

## gc_horizon (SharedMemoryLayout)
- gc_horizon is a monotonically increasing CommitSeq safe-point: min(begin_seq) across all active txns
- Since begin_seq derives from monotonically increasing published commit_seq, gc_horizon never decreases
- gc_horizon is authoritative ONLY when advanced by the commit sequencer (other processes read-only)

## GC Scheduling Policy (Alien-Artifact)
- f_gc = min(f_max, max(f_min, version_chain_pressure / target_chain_length))
- f_max = 100 Hz (never GC more often than 10ms)
- f_min = 1 Hz (always GC at least once per second)
- version_chain_pressure = observed mean chain length (BOCPD-tracked)
- target_chain_length = 8 (from Theorem 5: R*D+1 for R=100, D=0.07s)
- WHO runs GC: commit coordinator runs raise_gc_horizon() after each group commit batch
- Only the process holding WAL write lock (coordinator) runs GC -- avoids thundering herd
- Other processes observe updated gc_horizon on their next read

## raise_gc_horizon() Algorithm (normative)
- Default: if no active txns, safe point = latest commit_seq
- Scan all TxnSlots:
  - Skip tid==0 (empty)
  - CRITICAL: Sentinel-tagged slots (CLAIMING/CLEANING) are horizon blockers
    - Use min(global_min_begin_seq, old_horizon) for sentinel slots
    - Reason: claiming slot may have captured snapshot but not published real txn_id yet
  - For real TxnId slots: min with slot.begin_seq
- new_horizon = max(old_horizon, global_min_begin_seq) -- monotonic
- Store with Release ordering

## In-Process Version Pruning (§5.6.5.1, REQUIRED)
- Advancing gc_horizon defines reclaimable versions (Theorem 4) but doesn't reclaim memory
- MUST implement incremental, touched-page-driven pruning with strict work budgets
- FORBIDDEN: naive scan-everything-under-VersionArena-write-guard (stop-the-world pauses)

### GcTodo Queue
- GcTodo { queue: VecDeque<PageNumber>, in_queue: HashSet<PageNumber> }
- on_publish_or_materialize_version(pgno): enqueue if not already present
- gc_tick(): pop pages from queue and prune their version chains

### Work Budgets (normative)
- pages_budget = 64
- versions_budget = 4096
- Lock VersionArena.write() only during actual pruning work

### prune_page_chain(pgno, horizon) Algorithm
- Walk chain from head down through versions newer than horizon
- Find committed version <= horizon -> becomes new tail
- Everything older is reclaimable by Theorem 4
- Sever chain: arena[cur].prev_idx = None
- Free all nodes beyond the severed point to free list

### ARC Interaction (normative)
- When committed version removed from chain, its cache entry MUST be eviction-eligible
- Remove (pgno, commit_seq) from ARC indexes and ghost lists (§6.7 coalescing + §6.6 durability)

### I/O Boundary (normative)
- prune_page_chain is pure in-memory work, MUST NOT perform file reads
- If pruned version later needed by old snapshot, resolve() consults durable store (§5.2, §7.11)

PARENT EPIC: bd-3t3
DEPENDS ON: bd-3t3.1 (Core Types), bd-3t3.2 (Invariants/Visibility), bd-3t3.4 (Safety Proofs/Theorem 4-5)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:40:25.644030688Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T17:39:21.294486077Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T06:20:12.463097311Z","close_reason":"Content merged into bd-zcdn","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1s71","depends_on_id":"bd-3t3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1s71","depends_on_id":"bd-3t3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1s71","depends_on_id":"bd-3t3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1s71","depends_on_id":"bd-3t3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1864,"issue_id":"bd-1s71","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

- [ ] GC horizon correctly computed as min(snapshot.high for all active txns) using safe_gc_seq from shared memory
- [ ] Version pruning never reclaims versions needed by any active or future transaction (Theorem 4)
- [ ] Incremental pruning: prune_page_chain(pgno, horizon) walks chain, frees versions older than horizon
- [ ] Work budget enforced: max 64 pages and 4096 versions per GC tick
- [ ] Pruning is pure in-memory operation (no file I/O during GC)
- [ ] GC scheduling responsive to BOCPD regime shifts (adjusts frequency on workload change)
- [ ] Monotonic coarseness: GC horizon only advances, never retreats
- [ ] Chain tail after pruning: no version older than safe_gc_seq remains
- [ ] Pruning integrates with ARC cache: evicted pages also have chains pruned
","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:52:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1s7a","title":"Wire SQL execution path to storage-backed VDBE cursors (replace MemDatabase scan path)","description":"Close the core architecture gap where `fsqlite-core` executes SQL against `MemDatabase` while storage crates (`fsqlite-pager`, `fsqlite-btree`, `fsqlite-wal`, `fsqlite-mvcc`) are not yet wired into the runtime cursor path.

Scope (first slice):
- Wire a real storage-backed table scan path for simple SELECT execution:
  - OpenRead/Rewind/Column/Next in `fsqlite-vdbe` should be able to read rows from a B-tree cursor backed by pager pages.
- Keep existing in-memory behavior as test fallback only where explicitly needed.
- Add deterministic tests proving persisted rows are read through the storage path after reopen.

Why this is high-impact:
- It connects the SQL pipeline to the actual persistence engine.
- It unlocks meaningful end-to-end validation of MVCC/pager/wal behavior through normal query execution.

## Unit Test Requirements
- test_vdbe_openread_uses_btree_cursor_backend
- test_select_uses_storage_cursor_not_memdb_for_persisted_table
- test_reopen_then_select_reads_persisted_rows_via_storage_path

## E2E Test
- test_e2e_sql_pipeline_storage_backed_select_roundtrip

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `Connection` no longer relies on `MemDatabase` for the supported simple SELECT storage path.
- [ ] VDBE cursor opcodes required for table scan (`OpenRead`, `Rewind`, `Column`, `Next`) execute against storage-backed cursor state.
- [ ] Persisted data survives close/reopen and is returned by SELECT through the storage path.
- [ ] New tests are deterministic and pass under `cargo test -p fsqlite-core` and related crate tests.","notes":"2026-02-10: First implementation slice landed. VDBE OpenRead/Rewind/Next/Prev/Column/Rowid/SeekRowid now support storage-backed cursor bridge via MockBtreeCursor when enabled. Connection now enables storage-read cursors for table program execution. Rowid-seek codegen bug fixed (SeekRowid now receives rowid register). Added targeted tests in fsqlite-vdbe and fsqlite-core. Remaining: replace MemDatabase fallback with true pager/btree-backed persistence path and cover close/reopen persisted roundtrip acceptance criteria.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:51:48.138832762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T02:04:33.253290582Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T02:04:33.253268621Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria satisfied: (1) test_vdbe_openread_uses_btree_cursor_backend passes, (2) test_select_uses_storage_cursor_not_memdb_for_persisted_table passes, (3) test_reopen_then_select_reads_persisted_rows_via_storage_path passes, (4) test_e2e_sql_pipeline_storage_backed_select_roundtrip passes.
